preview.summary: Two sides chasing an unexpected route out of a Belgium- and Egypt-led Group G meet in a game both will privately view as their best shot at points. Iran bring Asian pedigree and a structured, counter-attacking shape, while New Zealand will look to make the most of a 16-year return to the World Cup stage. Expect a cagey, physical contest where the first goal carries outsized weight.
preview.homeForm: Comfortable AFC qualifiers and a settled defensive block, sitting around 21st in the FIFA rankings.
preview.awayForm: Back at a World Cup after 16 years via Oceania's first direct slot; the lowest-ranked side in the field at 85th.
preview.homeKey: Mehdi Taremi, captain and Olympiacos forward, Iran's main goal threat
preview.awayKey: Chris Wood, the 34-year-old captain and all-time top scorer
storylines: Iran missing Sardar Azmoun, omitted from the final squad
New Zealand's first World Cup appearance since 2010
Both sides know points here may decide who fights for second behind Belgium
preview.h2h: The teams have met only a handful of times in friendlies, with no competitive history between them.
